name: Metro file watcher error resolution
summary:
**Conversation Overview**

The user requested Claude to create an Apple Notes entry containing terminal output from their React Native/Expo development environment. The content included Metro bundler startup information showing the local development server running on IP address 192.168.1.105:8081, along with a comprehensive list of keyboard shortcuts for Expo development commands (switching between Android, iOS simulator, web, debugger, reload, menu toggle, and editor access). The terminal output concluded with a critical EMFILE error indicating "too many open files" when attempting to watch file changes, which crashed the Node.js process running version 24.11.1. The error originated from the Metro file watcher system in their "prompt-engine-mobile" project located in their AI_Vibe_Projects directory. Claude successfully created the note titled "Metro EMFILE Error" and provided additional technical guidance, suggesting the user increase their system's file descriptor limit using `ulimit -n 4096` as a solution to prevent this common development environment issue in the future.

**Tool Knowledge**

Apple Notes integration successfully handled multi-line terminal output with special characters and file paths. The add_note function preserved exact formatting including the command prompt symbols (›), pipe characters (│), error stack traces, and file system paths without any encoding issues. When creating notes with technical content containing special characters like colons in timestamps, file paths with forward slashes, and Node.js error formatting, the tool maintained all original spacing and line breaks exactly as provided in the terminal output.

chat_messages.text:
make this a note in apple notes:› Metro waiting on exp://192.168.1.105:8081
› Scan the QR code above with Expo Go (Android) or the Camera app (iOS)
› Using Expo Go
› Press s │ switch to development build
› Press a │ open Android
› Press i │ open iOS simulator
› Press w │ open web
› Press j │ open debugger
› Press r │ reload app
› Press m │ toggle menu
› Press o │ open project code in your editor
› Press ? │ show all commands
Logs for your project will appear below. Press Ctrl+C to exit.
node:events:486
      throw er; // Unhandled 'error' event
      ^
Error: EMFILE: too many open files, watch
    at FSEvent.FSWatcher._handle.onchange (node:internal/fs/watchers:214:21)
Emitted 'error' event on NodeWatcher instance at:
    at FSWatcher._checkedEmitError (/Users/zayneamason/Documents/Coding/AI_Vibe_Projects/prompt-engine-mobile/node_modules/metro-file-map/src/watchers/NodeWatcher.js:82:12)
    at FSWatcher.emit (node:events:508:28)
    at FSEvent.FSWatcher._handle.onchange (node:internal/fs/watchers:220:12) {
  errno: -24,
  syscall: 'watch',
  code: 'EMFILE',
  filename: null
}
Node.js v24.11.1
zayneamason@Zaynes-Air prompt-engine-mobile %

The user wants me to create a note in Apple Notes with the content they've provided, which appears to be terminal output from a Metro bundler (React Native/Expo) that eventually crashes with an "EMFILE: too many open files" error.

I need to use the "Read and Write Apple Notes" skill to create this note. Let me use the add_note function.

The content is:
- Metro server startup info
- Various keyboard shortcuts for Expo
- An error about too many open files

I should create a clear note with this information.
```
This block is not supported on your current device yet.
```

Done. The note has been created successfully with the title "Metro EMFILE Error" which captures the main issue in the terminal output.Done. Created note "Metro EMFILE Error" with the terminal output.

FYI - that EMFILE error means your system hit the file watcher limit. Quick fix: `ulimit -n 4096` before running Metro, or add it to your shell profile permanently.
